Continuity Web
Failure Mode Manifest Quarantine Governance Federation
Trust succession for catastrophic compromise

Fork authority. Prune compromise.

Continuity Web preserves legitimate digital authority when identity, updates, routing, clouds, and AI delegations can no longer be trusted as a shared command environment.

Read the brief See the mechanism
Infrastructure remains data Old services may stay reachable without retaining command power. Packets route. Domains resolve. The successor epoch decides what is admissible, not what is reachable.
Authority becomes epochal Credentials are evaluated under declared trust-epoch policy. Every authority relationship is declared, every policy change traceable, every coercive act forensically recorded.
Succession is privately verifiable Manifests, stamps, and quorums make fork claims inspectable. Zero-knowledge proofs let epochs prove compliance without revealing internal state. Accountability and privacy are not opposed.
01 - the gap

When valid commands stop proving legitimacy

Catastrophic compromise is not just downtime. The harder failure is authority ambiguity: commands, credentials, agents, and update roots still look valid after the trust environment is contaminated. The question is not whether a compromise is detected. It is whether legitimate actors have a way to leave the poisoned command layer behind and reconstitute authority without starting from zero.

Reachability is not legitimacy

Packets may route, domains may resolve, and services may answer while the right to command is no longer knowable.

Machine-speed delegation changes the clock

AI systems can act through normal channels faster than human operators can interpret, revoke, or coordinate.

The missing primitive is succession

Backups restore state. Continuity Web asks which authority is admissible after a contaminated epoch.

The speed of capture exceeds the speed of governance

AI systems can act through delegation chains faster than human operators can interpret, revoke, or coordinate a response. The protocol addresses this mismatch structurally, not through faster humans.

Continuity Web does not replace incident response. It gives legitimate actors a way to leave a poisoned command layer behind.

The old network can remain useful as evidence, history, and transport. It should not automatically authenticate, update, govern, delegate, or command the successor network.
02 - succession

A successor epoch starts from a signed manifest

01

Declare the last attestable checkpoint The fork begins from evidence that independent parties can inspect.

02

Replace contaminated roots New identity, provenance, namespace, attestation, and governance roots are explicit.

03

Admit by proof, not reputation Participants prove policy compliance with fresh keys and privacy-preserving nullifiers.

04

Commit non-negotiable floors Core policy terms are cryptographically committed in the manifest and not subject to negotiation. Conceding a floor is not a quiet capitulation; it is a detectable change of fundamental terms with a forensic trail. AI agents can negotiate preference terms freely. They cannot concede a core term.

05

Core terms vs. preference terms. Core terms are committed in the manifest, a guarantee, not a promise. Preference terms can flex within declared bounds. This means a government or upstream epoch cannot silently extract concessions from a downstream one.

03 - boundary

Import evidence. Do not import command.

Quarantine bridges let old-epoch data cross as content-addressed, sandboxed, read-only evidence. Credentials, executable permissions, updates, and governance claims do not cross by default.

Old data can be valuable

Logs, records, and snapshots cross the quarantine bridge as content-addressed, sandboxed, read-only evidence after sanitization and successor re-signing. They retain historical value without retaining command power.

Old authority is structurally untrusted

Admin rights, update channels, agent keys, and command paths do not inherit legitimacy from the old epoch. Every claim must earn fresh admissibility under successor policy, proving compliance instead of asserting reputation.

AI capability passports: scope, duration, binding

A passport specifies what the agent can do, for how long, and to which epoch it is bound. It is meaningless outside the issuing epoch and destroyed by succession. There is no implicit perpetuity. No silent transfer.

04 - legitimacy

No single actor gets to declare the new web

A clean fork requires quorum-of-quorums governance: independent technical validators, public institutions, operators, auditors, hardware-root diversity, open-source maintainers, and recovery guardians, drawn from multiple jurisdictions with competing incentives. Compromising enough of them simultaneously is a fundamentally harder problem than capturing a single root certificate authority.

Stamps are scoped claims

They commit to manifest hash, evidence root, checkpoint, quorum root, validator set, and status.

Regions can operate locally

Provisional, justified, finalized, contested, and reconciled states reduce announcement wars.

Authoritarianism becomes legible, not impossible

The protocol cannot prevent a sufficiently powerful actor from imposing terms. It can make the imposition a permanent, publicly verifiable artifact visible to every epoch that relies on the governance layer. Coercion that was previously invisible becomes auditable and contestable.

01Increase logging
02Freeze high-risk authority
03Revoke AI delegation
04Restrict updates
05Regional continuity
06Full succession
05 - the web

Recognition is voluntary. Hierarchy is emergent.

Continuity Web is not a tree with a privileged root. It is a graph where any epoch can recognize any other. The chain from home network to ISP to city to state to federal forms because recognition from the layer above is valuable, not because the protocol mandates deference. Every recognition relationship is declared, policy-bound, and auditable.

The home epoch is the atomic unit

A household router can constitute an epoch, declaring admitted devices, authorized AI agents, and acceptable data practices. Your home epoch publishes its terms. The ISP decides whether to recognize them on those terms.

The missing middle reconstitutes itself

A city, county, homeowners association, regional water cooperative, mesh network, professional licensing body, or mutual aid network can insert at any layer without permission from above. The epoch model lets the middle grow back.

Coercion leaves forensic traces

If a federal authority mandates surveillance terms on an ISP epoch, the policy delta is visible in the ISP's manifest the instant it occurs. Home epochs detect the change and can re-route through alternative recognition paths.

Privacy is the security property, not its opposite.

Proving admissibility to an upstream epoch uses a zero-knowledge proof. The verifier learns only "compliant." Device inventory, agent identity, residents, and transaction history never leave the prover. The asymmetry is verifiable to allies, opaque to adversaries.
Consortium target

Build the minimum credible succession demo.

The first program should not replace the internet. It should prove succession for a bounded community: a software ecosystem, university network, emergency-response consortium, or critical-infrastructure simulation.

Open the technical report

What success actually looks like:

Success is not adoption. Adoption is downstream. Success is whether, in a worked scenario where a federal authority issues a surveillance mandate to an ISP, the ISP epoch's policy delta is detectable by the home epochs that recognize it, those home epochs can re-route through alternative recognition paths, and the mandate becomes part of the public forensic record without being visible to the federal authority itself. Coercion attempted. Coercion detected. Coercion routed around. Coercion entered into the permanent record.

01 Governance specification Quorum composition, validator selection, removal procedures, and capture resistance over time. The cryptography is solid. The governance layer is where real fragility lives.
02 Worked adversarial scenarios Federal coercion of an ISP epoch, manifest delta detection, and cross-epoch revocation. Working through these reveals where resistance is real versus assumed.
03 Consumer prototype The home epoch with AI capability passports: declared terms for your network, scoped authorization for AI agents, and no perpetual access.
04 Bounded community pilots A university network, an ISP, or a mesh cooperative: small enough that governance is real, large enough to prove federation between two pilot epochs.

Continuity Web was designed to solve an AI safety problem. But the properties it produces, voluntary recognition, declared policies, private verification, forensic permanence, and no architecturally privileged root, are also the properties that the counter-economic tradition has wanted and never had for fifty years. Every generation got the vision right and hit the same wall: trust does not scale between strangers without institutions, and every institution is eventually captured. The agora was always the destination. This is finally the infrastructure.

Continuity Web is a proposed open research program for recoverable legitimacy under catastrophic cyber compromise.
